observations.summary: Launchdeck is a Node.js CLI tool implementing a daemonless, user-scoped global control plane for local project services
The project uses JavaScript ESM running on Node.js >=20 with zero non-standard runtime dependencies beyond 'yaml' for config parsing
Launchdeck has 16 source files, 20 test files, comprehensive documentation, and follows Spec Kit Plus workflow conventions
CLI has approximately 30+ commands organized into lifecycle, process management, registry, observability, clean, and agent domains
Control plane architecture: actions, events, inspect, locks, ownership, runs, and state modules working together with global-runtime and project-local runtime
Key safety invariants: never kill unowned processes, ownership proof required for stop/restart/force-stop, clean is hygiene-only, project remove refuses active runs
Project uses node:test (Node built-in test runner) with no external test framework dependency
JSON output uses stable envelope with schemaVersion, next actions; compact mode available for agent-friendly output
Cross-platform CI runs on Windows, macOS, Linux with syntax check, tests, and lifecycle smoke verification
Two-level runtime: global-runtime manages user-level project registry, while project-local runtime manages per-project state and processes
Agent skill installer supports 4 adapters: codex, claude-code, github-copilot, visual-studio with project-scoped and user-scoped installation
Design system defined in DESIGN.md with color tokens, spacing scale, typography, component states, and accessibility requirements
Project uses .agents/ directory for canonical agent skills, not .claude/skills or .codex/skills (those are synced copies)
scan-set resolved 523 candidate files, classified as P0:16, P1:29, P2:47, P3:431
P3 files (431) are primarily .claude/ and .codex/ skill copies - vendored tooling, not project source
Config discovery searches upward from current directory for .launchdeck.yml/.yaml or launchdeck.yml/.yaml, the containing directory becomes projectRoot
Process ownership is critical to safety: Launchdeck must prove it owns a process before stop/restart/force-stop; port or PID evidence alone is insufficient
